tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: After Four Final Heartbreaks, Zverev Finally Wins Grand Slam—Collapses in Tears
stories.summary: Alexander Zverev finally wins his first Grand Slam at Roland Garros, breaking down in tears after four devastating final losses, with the fifth set victory ending years of heartbreak on clay.

stories.headline: Seven-Year-Old's Pillow Project Sparks Statewide Kindness Movement
stories.summary: Current summary unchanged due to article excerpt containing no relevant details about Ryan's pillow project or the governor's statewide initiative.

stories.headline: Elderly Dementia Patient Recovers Speech and Mobility After Psilocybin Treatment
stories.summary: An elderly woman in her 80s with advanced Alzheimer's temporarily regained the ability to speak beyond monosyllables and bladder control after taking high-dose psilocybin mushrooms in Brazil.

stories.headline: After 150 Years, Salmon and Lamprey Come Home to Restored Estuary
stories.summary: After 15 years of planning, the Siuslaw Estuary's 217 acres reconnect to the river as a levee breaches, welcoming salmon and lamprey home to a former dairy site for the first time in 150 years.

stories.headline: Beloved White Toki Birds Soar Over Japan Again After Four Decades
stories.summary: Eight crested ibises soared over Japan's Noto region on May 31, returning to mountains where they vanished in the 1970s, with Crown Prince Akishino celebrating their release.

stories.headline: First-Ever Multi-Organ Transplant From Pig Gives Hope to Thousands Waiting
stories.summary: A patient in China received two kidneys and a whole liver from a genetically modified pig in the first-ever multi-organ xenotransplant, showing no immediate rejection.

stories.headline: Black Rhinos Return to Zimbabwe Park After 30 Years of Absence
stories.summary: Seventeen endangered black rhinos arrived in Zimbabwe's Matusadona National Park, the first in 30 years, with park manager Michael Pelham calling it an "incredible closing of a circle."

stories.headline: Cancer-Fighting Cells Get Built-In GPS to Hunt Down Hidden Tumors
stories.summary: Researchers at the Max Delbrück Center engineered CAR-T cells with the CCR7 receptor, allowing them to navigate lymph nodes and more effectively kill lymphoma cells.

stories.headline: Vietnam's Secret Sanctuary Harbors 90% of World's Rarest Langurs
stories.summary: Vietnam's Ninh Binh mountains shelter more than 90 percent of the world's critically endangered Delacour's langurs, jet-black primates with distinctive white hip patches thriving through local conservation.

stories.headline: NASA Engineer's Propellantless Space Drive Opens New Frontier for Exploration
stories.summary: Charles Buhler, a veteran NASA engineer who worked on the Space Shuttle, Hubble, and International Space Station, says his team built a propulsion system generating thrust without any fuel.
